sections.paragraph:
fut. יֶחְפַּר once in plur. תַּחְפְּרוּ, Isa 1:29. Arabic خَفَِر to blush, to be ashamed. (Perhaps kindred to חָמַר No. 2, to be red.) Mostly used of shame arising from disappointed hope, Psa 35:4, 26 Psa 35:26 40:15 70:3 83:18 with the addition of פָּנִים Psa 34:6 Job 11:18, וְחָפַרְתָּ לָבֶטַח תִּשְׁכָּב “(now) thou art ashamed, (afterwards), thou shalt dwell in tranquillity;” followed by מִן of the thing in which any one is disappointed, Isa 1:29 (comp. בֹּוש).

Hiphil-

(1) to put to shame, to cause dishonour, Pro 13:5 19:26 .

(2) intrans. like Kal (compare verbs of colour). Isa 54:4 spoken of Mount Lebanon, Isa 33:9.
